Returns the template object with all metadata and content fields. Useful for previewing or validating template structure before sending transactional emails.

Enhanced version clarifies the operation retrieves a specific template by ID, explains the return structure, and provides context for when an AI agent would use this operation (e.g., before sending emails).

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of email contacts that are blocked or have unsubscribed from transactional emails. Supports filtering and sorting through query parameters. Does not modify any contact data.

data.summary_suggestion: Delete a transactional email log entry
data.description_suggestion: Deletes a specific transactional email log entry identified by the provided identifier. The identifier can be the email message ID or log record ID. This operation permanently removes the log record from your account. Side effects: The deleted log entry will no longer appear in email statistics, reports, or event queries. This action cannot be undone.
data.reasoning: Original description is minimal and doesn't explain what identifier is required, what the side effects are, or whether deletion is permanent. data.current_summary: Get the list of email templates

data.description_suggestion: Creates a new email template in your account. Requires template name, subject line, and HTML/text content in the request body. message: Operation 'delete-smtp-template' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_side_effects. Original description mentions the inactive requirement but lacks clarity about how to identify templates, what happens if you try to delete an active template, and the consequences of deletion. data.description_suggestion: Deletes an email template identified by templateId. Only inactive templates can be deleted; active templates must be deactivated first. Requires the template ID as a path parameter. This operation permanently removes the template from your account. Side effects: The deleted template can no longer be used for sending transactional emails. Any references to this template ID in future sends will fail.
data.reasoning: Original description mentions the inactive requirement but lacks clarity about how to identify templates, what happens if you try to delete an active template, and the consequences of deletion. data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing email template identified by templateId. Supports partial updates - only provided fields in the request body will be modified (name, subject, HTML content, text content, etc.). Requires template ID as a path parameter. Returns the updated template object with all current properties. Side effects: Changes take effect immediately for new email sends using this template. Existing sent emails are not affected. Active templates can be updated without deactivation.

data.current_description: Delete hardbounces. To use carefully (e.g. in case of temporary ISP failures)
data.summary_suggestion: Remove hardbounced email addresses from blocklist
data.description_suggestion: Removes email addresses from the hardbounce blocklist, allowing them to receive future emails. Use this operation carefully and only when you are certain the bounce was temporary (e.g., due to ISP failures or misconfiguration) rather than a permanent delivery failure. Accepts a list of email addresses to remove from the hardbounce list. This operation permanently modifies the blocklist and should be used judiciously to avoid sending to invalid addresses.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description is vague about what 'delete hardbounces' means operationally and lacks input/output details. The warning about careful use is present but not well-contextualized. Enhanced version clarifies the operation's effect (removing from blocklist), provides context for when to use it, explains the input requirement, and emphasizes the permanent nature of the change for better AI agent judgment.
